other_energy_programs.name: Energy Efficiency, Renewables, and Grid Operations

2026.methodology: The 2026 forecast uses the official FY 2026 Budget Request as the primary baseline. Total Income ($51.12B) is calculated as $46.32B in base discretionary authority plus projected $4.8B from defense reconciliation for NNSA. Spending categories reflect the 'Energy Dominance' policy shift: a 24.4% increase for NNSA to $30.04B, while Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) programs are reduced by 74% to approximately $0.88B. Research spending is realigned toward AI, fusion, and high-speed computing within the Office of Science.

sources: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) Supplemental Funding
Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) Title V Provisions
Department of Energy Federal Credit Support

clean_energy_and_grid.name: Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) & Grid Deployment

2025.methodology: Figures represent the 2025 Fiscal Year estimated budgetary resources. Total income includes the requested $51.1B discretionary base plus approximately $8B in specialized mandatory funding from BIL/IRA. Spending distribution is based on the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) functional classifications, emphasizing the continued shift towards clean energy deployment and nuclear stockpile modernization. Values are non-rounded to reflect precise statutory requests.

sources: Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024

science_and_innovation.name: Office of Science and Energy Programs

infrastructure_and_loans.name: Grid Deployment and Loan Programs Office

2024.methodology: Data derived from the FY 2024 Enacted Budget Authority and USAspending.gov actual outlay reports. Income is defined as total budgetary resources including discretionary funds ($50.1B) and mandatory/reimbursable authority. Spending reflects total obligations and net outlays. Percentages calculated based on the $51.99B total authority to ensure internal consistency across NNSA, Science, and Environmental tracks.
